summary: Thrilling set with soaring Believe It Or Not and thunderous Morning Dew
blurb: Features a standout debut of 'Believe It Or Not', powerful 'Morning Dew' climax, and tight 'Hey Pocky Way' opener. Jerry’s vocals and guitar shine, while Brent adds vibrant backing harmonies throughout.
review: June 23, 1988 at Alpine Valley delivered one of the late‑80s’ most memorable nights. The show kicked off with a bright ‘Hey Pocky Way’, instantly setting an upbeat tone that carried through a crisp first block of classics – ‘It Must Have Been The Roses’, ‘When I Paint My Masterpiece’, and a seamless segue into ‘The Promised Land’.  The second set was the real high‑water mark: the band unveiled the rarely‑heard ‘Believe It Or Not’, with Jerry’s voice “emotional” and his guitar tone shimmering over Brent’s soaring backing vocals.  That momentum surged into a thunderous ‘Morning Dew’ that felt like a summer sunrise, followed by a tight drum‑and‑space excursion that was described as “awesome” and “lysergic”.  The encore closed on a playful note with the infamous Blackbird blooper and a gentle ‘Brokedown Palace’.  Across all available tapes the band sounds locked in – Jerry’s vocals are powerful (aside from an occasional flanging effect), Phil provides his trademark “thunder bass”, Brent is a vocal/keyboard powerhouse, and Bill/Kreutzmann drive the rhythm with punchy kick drums and inventive textures.  The soundboard capture chosen as the best recording balances clarity with the live ambience, making this Alpine Valley show a must‑listen for anyone craving that late‑80s Dead energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four out of five recordings are positive, repeatedly praising the debut of 'Believe It Or Not', the thunderous 'Morning Dew', and Brent's vocal contributions. Consistent language like 'absolutely incredible', 'thunder bass', and 'awesome' across multiple sources indicates strong positive sentiment.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with good overall clarity, balanced mix and enough bass/drum definition to capture the band's dynamics despite minor vocal flanging.

key_highlights: Debut of the rarely‑played ‘Believe It Or Not’
Thunderous ‘Morning Dew’ that lifts the second set
Tight, energetic ‘Hey Pocky Way’ opener with Brent’s backing vocals
Powerful Drums & Space jam creating an elemental climax

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers emotional vocals on 'Believe It Or Not' and wailing guitar on 'Morning Dew'; overall tone is powerful with only occasional timing slips.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a deep, thunder‑bass foundation; big chord work during the Dew and generally solid playing despite minor rhythm tightness issues.
band_performance.Bob: Energy stays high; lead vocals shine on 'All Along The Watchtower' and he shares in the on‑stage laughter during the Blackbird blooper.
band_performance.Brent: Stands out with vibrant backing vocals, scat on 'Hey Pocky Way', and lively keyboard textures throughout; his humor adds to the show's communal vibe.
band_performance.Billy: Drums are punchy and clear; the Drums & Space segment is highlighted as ‘awesome’, showcasing inventive percussion textures.
